title: Cactus × mallisonii
intro: Cactus × mallisonii, also known as the Night-Blooming Cereus, is a hybrid plant species between Selenicereus grandiflorus and Selenicereus thomsonii. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its antipyretic, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cactus × mallisonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cactus × mallisonii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Cactus × mallisonii is a naturally occurring hybrid of two species within the Cactaceae family. It is believed to be a cross between either Ferocactus alamosanus and Echinocereus pentalophus or Ferocactus alamosanus and Echinocereus triglochidiatus. The hybrid nature of Cactus × mallisonii is thought to have evolved as a result of the geographical isolation of the parent species. This hybridization can lead to the creation of unique characteristics in the resulting plant. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and origin of Cactus × mallisonii.
